tactical_shape: fast and loose in buildup play
responded with a powerhouse second-half display
standout_players: Harry Kane 9/10 — scored twice and equalled England’s World Cup finals scoring record; played like a quarterback at times
Jude Bellingham 7/10 — lost the ball for Croatia’s first goal but then scored a brilliant match-turning goal
Noni Madueke 8/10 — won the penalty and was very dangerous down the right flank
Elliot Anderson 8/10 — kept things ticking over and assisted Bellingham
Declan Rice 7/10 — delivered a pinpoint corner for Kane’s header, though not at his dominant best
Jordan Pickford 6/10 — shaky overall but made a crucial late save
Marcus Rashford 7/10 — came off the bench and finished clinically
Bukayo Saka 7/10 — returned from injury concerns and helped create the clincher
Nico O’Reilly 7/10 — got forward well, work in progress defensively
fatigue_injuries: Declan Rice looked fatigued after a long season
Bukayo Saka was a surprise substitute given injury concerns
home_read.momentum: England are up and running after thriving under pressure and showing they have too much for Croatia.

tactical_shape: open and generous defending at times
standout_players: Dominik Livakovic 9/10 — produced unbelievable stops and kept Croatia in it
Martin Baturina 7/10 — took his goal brilliantly and caused England problems
Petar Musa 7/10 — scored neatly and was a real handful
Petar Sucic 7/10 — supplied a brilliant assist for Baturina
Ivan Perisic 7/10 — clever header for the second equaliser and still full of energy
Josip Stanisic 7/10 — lively on the right and often got the better of O’Reilly
Luka Modric 7/10 — gave away the early penalty but still oozed class
away_read.momentum: Croatia showed resilience and threat in a high-quality contest, but England’s power and depth eventually told.
